title: EIP-8120: MLOAD8 and CALLDATALOAD8 Opcodes
description: Introduces two new EVM opcodes, MLOAD8 and CALLDATALOAD8, for loading a single byte from memory or calldata in one operation. Currently, reading a single byte requires a 32-byte load followed by a bit shift, costing 9 gas and extra bytecode. These opcodes reduce per-byte reads to 3 gas and save approximately 3 bytes of deployed bytecode per occurrence.
author: Helkomine (@Helkomine)
laymanDescription: Adds two new instructions to Ethereum's virtual machine that let smart contracts read a single byte of data more cheaply. Today this requires multiple steps; these new instructions do it in one step, saving gas fees and making contracts smaller.

endUsers.description: No direct impact. Contracts using byte-oriented parsing may become slightly cheaper to call due to reduced gas consumption.

appDevs.description: Smart contract developers gain efficient single-byte access opcodes, reducing gas costs and bytecode size for byte-oriented parsing logic.

walletDevs.description: No impact. These opcodes operate at the EVM level and do not change transaction formats or wallet interfaces.

toolingInfra.description: Debuggers, disassemblers, and EVM tooling must add support for the two new opcodes and their semantics.

layer2s.description: L2 chains running EVM-compatible execution environments would need to implement the new opcodes to maintain compatibility.

stakersNodes.description: No direct impact beyond running updated client software that supports the new opcodes after activation.

elClients.description: Must implement two new opcodes with single-byte load semantics, memory expansion rules for MLOAD8, and appropriate gas metering. Low complexity.

clClients.description: No consensus client changes required. This is purely an EVM execution layer change requiring only fork-activation awareness.

benefits: Reduces gas cost for single-byte reads from 9 gas to 3 gas.
Shrinks deployed bytecode by approximately 3 bytes per single-byte access.
Completes opcode symmetry by pairing MLOAD8 with the existing MSTORE8.
Benefits contracts that parse byte-oriented calldata or instruction streams.

scaleL1.description: Reduces gas consumption and bytecode size for byte-oriented contract operations, improving L1 execution efficiency.
